subject: [PATCH v3 03/11] mm: handle PMD swap entries in fork path

2026-07-03.patch_summary: This patch improves the handling of PMD (Page Map Directory) swap entries during the fork process in Linux. It introduces a new function, `swap_dup_entries_direct`, which can duplicate a contiguous range of swap slots in one call. The patch also modifies the `copy_huge_non_present_pmd` function to 'copy' PMD swap entries instead of splitting them, preserving the Huge Page (THP). This change mirrors the behavior of `copy_nonpresent_pte` for PTEs and ensures that the PMD is stable across retries if necessary. The patch also includes modifications to handle GFP_ATOMIC table allocation failures and retries with GFP_KERNEL.
